type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Jungbunzlauer Canada Inc. acquired a facility owned by Ingredion Canada Corporation on Dec 15, 2015.  The acquired facility (NPRI ID: 10303) is located at 55 Invertose Drive East, Port Colborne, ON L3K 5X7 Canada.  The NAICS code representing the operations in acquired facility is 311221 - Wet Corn Milling.  
The acquired facility has reported to the program in the past, reported information is reported under Jungbunzlauer Canada Inc. going forward.  As a result, emissions from Jungbunzlauer Canada Inc. increased from previous reporting year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Increased optimization / de-nitrification efforts to try and lower the amount of nitrate generated from the nitrification process and discharged as nitrate ion in solution within the treated process effluent. Optimization on nutrient additions of aqueous ammonia into the WWTP. TRA target of ammonia and nitrate reductions were implemented.

treated more wastewater from the manufacturing processes in 2014

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increased optimization / de-nitrification efforts to try and lower the amount of nitrate generated from the nitrification process and discharged as nitrate ion in solution within the treated process effluent. Optimization on nutrient additions of aqueous ammonia into the WWTP. TRA target of ammonia and nitrate reductions were implemented.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Spill of 13,000 L of 26% Aqua Ammonia into containment trench at the WWTP due to tank flange break. Spill occurred February 17th, 2012 @ ~12:45 PM. The tank was topped off the same morning without issue.  The ammonia tank was repaired same night and contents were pumped back into storage tank. Vapour release due to the spill was calculated as per Ontario Ministry of Environment Spills Equation.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The change in quantity released from previous year is less than 10%.

The following errors in on-site release calculations for Hydrochloric acid (HCL) were identified which lead to an under estimate of on-site HCL releases for the 2011 reporting year:

HCL releases to air from facility HCL operations were not included in on-site releases. This was corrected and HCL releases to air are now included in on-site air releases.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The following errors in on-site release calculations for Ammonia (NH3) were identified which lead to an under estimate of on-site NH3 releases for the 2011 reporting year:

1)	Ammonia releases to air from facility operations were not included in on-site releases. This was corrected and ammonia releases to air are now included in on-site air releases.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Total phosphorus MPO amount was previously calculated incorrectly for the 2011 reporting year. Phosphorus component of phosphoric acid was not considered in the MPO amount. This was corrected and now phosphorus is a reportable substance.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The change in quantity released from previous year is less than 10%.

The following errors were identified and corrected in particulate matter on-site releases for the 2011 reporting year:

1) Road dust releases were incorrectly included in releases to air. Road dust releases were removed from on-site air releases.

2) Particulate Matter air releases from facility operations were not included in on-site releases to air. Particulate Matter air releases from facility operations are now included in releases to air.

3) Emission factors for natural gas combustion associated with comfort heaters were mistakenly used in the past. PM emissions were revised using emission factors associated with cogeneration units from chapter 3.1 of U.S EPA AP 42.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Water release of nitrate ion has decreased significantly. Less ammonia (as the nitrogen source) was added to balance the ratios of Carbon (COD) and phosphorous for effective treatment. Optimization / reduction strategy in effect for 2016. Operated the system slightly under stress due to minimized nutrient inputs.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: JBL has focused on reducing the amount of phosphorous in the process effluent. JBL achieved this by having minimal issues in the WWTP and by adding additional flocculants at the clarification stage of the wastewater treatment process.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Increase in air release:
Diluted spill from Aqueous Ammonia storage tank at the Glucose Plant. MECP SAC & Niagara District Office were notified. Spill was contained, cleaned up as soon as possible and utilized as a 																		    nutrient source within the on-site WWTP.  Other air release difference amounts less than 5%.
Decrease in water release:
Decrease in residual ammonia content in treated wastewater effluent.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Increase in nitrate content in treated wastewater effluent due to increased oxidation of ammonia and an increased nitrification capacity.
